articles.headline: Man United exploring all permanent manager options with Michael Carrick leading race - sources
articles.description: Manchester United are determined to explore all of their options before appointing their next permanent head coach despite caretaker boss Michael Carrick leading the race to get the job full-time, sources have told ESPN.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United eye West Ham left back
articles.description: Man United are keen on West Ham left back El Hadji Malick Diouf, while Liverpool line up a potential Mohamed Salah replacement. Transfer Talk has the latest.

articles.headline: Could Bruno Fernandes break long-standing Premier League assist record?
articles.description: Bruno Fernandes is on the verge of equalling the record for the most assists registered in a single Premier League season.

articles.headline: The earliest dates Arsenal or Man City could win the Premier League title
articles.description: The Premier League title race between Manchester City and Arsenal is set to go right down to the wire. But when could either side win it earliest?

articles.headline: Are Newcastle really in danger of being relegated from Premier League this season?
articles.description: Newcastle's poor run of form, and the results of their nearest rivals, has them in danger of getting sucked into the relegation conversation - are they safe from the drop?

articles.headline: What must happen for Man United to secure Champions League spot?
articles.description: Manchester United are on the verge of returning to the Champions League after a significant win over Brentford on Monday.

articles.headline: Premier League players out of contract: Who could leave your team on a free this summer?
articles.description: ESPN takes a look at the players who are out of contract in 2026 across all 20 Premier League clubs.

articles.headline: Are Tottenham going to be relegated from the Premier League? What stats, charts say
articles.description: For Tottenham fans, the team's nosedive into the relegation zone has forced them to think the unthinkable - are they really going to go down?

articles.headline: Arsenal and Man City's remaining Premier League fixtures compared as title race hots up
articles.description: The Premier League title race is well and truly underway with Arsenal being chased down by Pep Guardiola's Manchester City in their hunt for a first top-flight trophy since 2004.

articles.headline: Five times Arsenal have let the Premier League title slip away
articles.description: Arsenal's quest for their first Premier League title since the Invincibles' famous triumph in 2003-04 has recently taken a major dent.

articles.headline: Explained: Will UEFA ban Rice for comments after Atletico draw?
articles.description: Sky Sports' Dave Reed explains whether UEFA will ban Arsenal midfielder Declan Rice after his comments after the Atletico Madrid draw.

articles.headline: Papers: Carrick favourite for Man Utd job amid Iraola links
articles.description: The top stories and transfer rumours from Friday's newspapers...

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Barcelona eye move for Romero
articles.description: Cristian Romero could head to Barcelona next season with his future at Tottenham appearing uncertain. Transfer Talk has the latest.

articles.headline: Wood penalty gives Forest slender semi-final lead over Villa after VAR drama
articles.description: Chris Wood's penalty for Nottingham Forest gives them a 1-0 advantage over Aston Villa following the first leg of their Europa League semi-final – after VAR drama at the City Ground.

articles.headline: Why can't Premier League, all soccer be fun like PSG vs. Bayern?
articles.description: Tuesday's nine-goal thriller between PSG and Bayern Munich had all the makings of an instant (and all-time) classic. But there are reasons why games like this are an outlier, not the norm.

articles.headline: Tracking title races, relegation: Ronaldo nears Saudi trophy; when Barcelona can win LaLiga
articles.description: How are the battles for major honors, Champions League spots and relegation shaping up across the continent? Here's everything you need to know.

articles.headline: Ashley Young, 40, announces retirement at the end of the season
articles.description: Ashley Young has announced his retirement at the end of the season, following a career that has spanned 23 years and included spells at Manchester United, Aston Villa, Inter Milan and Everton.

articles.headline: Mauricio Pochettino: Tottenham downfall 'really sad'
articles.description: Former Tottenham manager Mauricio Pochettino has called the club's fall from grace 'really sad.'

articles.headline: Mohamed Salah to return before end of season for Liverpool farewell
articles.description: Mohamed Salah is expected to return to action before Liverpool's final game of the season, the club have said.

articles.headline: How Premier League's sixth place could earn a Champions League spot
articles.description: Newcastle United are 14th in the Premier League but, remarkably, Eddie Howe's side and every team above them can still hope to play in the Champions League next season.

articles.headline: Marcotti: I'm glad Salah gets to say goodbye properly after injury scare
articles.description: Gab and Juls react to the news that Mohamed Salah will return from injury before the end of his last season at Liverpool.

articles.headline: Marcotti: Ex-United players screaming Carrick should get the job is not enough
articles.description: Gab Marcotti says there needs to be "a proper review" before any decision is taken over who is the next Manchester United Manager.

articles.headline: Kobbie Mainoo signs new Man United contract
articles.description: Kobbie Mainoo has signed a new contract to extend his stay at Manchester United, the club have confirmed.

articles.headline: Burnley, Scott Parker part ways after Premier League relegation
articles.description: Manager Scott Parker has left Burnley following their relegation from the Premier League, the club has confirmed.

articles.headline: Mason Mount: Man United can challenge for Premier League title next season
articles.description: Mason Mount believes Manchester United boast the quality and mindset to challenge for the Premier League title next season.

articles.headline: VAR review: Should Arsenal have gotten second penalty vs. Atleti?
articles.description: Both Arsenal and Atletico Madrid got penalties in the first leg of their UEFA Champions League semifinal tie, but were the Gunners unjustly denied a second one?

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Barcelona looking at move for João Pedro
articles.description: Barcelona are reportedly looking to reinforce their attack by signing Chelsea striker João Pedro. Transfer Talk has the latest.

articles.headline: Nottingham Forest's Vitor Pereira keen to 'emulate' European Cup-winning Brian Clough
articles.description: Nottingham Forest boss Vitor Pereira has said he does not want a statue in the city centre, but hopes to emulate Brian Clough by bringing European glory to the City Ground.

articles.headline: Kerkez's debut Liverpool season: 'I know what I can do and why I am here'
articles.description: Milos Kerkez sits down with ESPN to reflect on his debut season at Liverpool, including his close friendship with departing legend Mohamed Salah.

articles.headline: Milos Kerkez: Liverpool will miss Mohamed Salah's professionalism and experience
articles.description: ESPN FC's Beth Lindop sits down with Liverpool's Milos Kerkez to discuss his dramatic first season at the club and his close relationship with Mohamed Salah.

articles.headline: Martin Ødegaard urges Arsenal to revel in 'amazing position'
articles.description: Arsenal skipper Martin Ødegaard has said his side are ready to go a step further in the title race.

articles.headline: Ødegaard: Arsenal want to make history this season
articles.description: Martin Ødegaard believes Arsenal have improved since last year's Champions League semifinal elimination.

articles.headline: Man City left frustrated by rescheduled fixtures amid title run-in - sources
articles.description: Manchester City have been left frustrated after being told by the Premier League they must play three games in seven days as they chase two trophies during a hectic end to the season.
